members.name: side_of_oriented_circle
members.brief: returns the relative position of point test to the oriented circle defined by p , q and r .
members.detailed: The order of the points p , q and r is important, since it determines the orientation of the implicitly constructed circle. If p , q and r are collinear, the circle degenerates in a line. CGAL::ON_ORIENTED_BOUNDARY is returned if test is also collinear or if two points are identical, otherwise, side_of_oriented_circle(r, q, test, p) is returned.
members.argsstring: (const CGAL::Point_2< Kernel > &p, const CGAL::Point_2< Kernel > &q, const CGAL::Point_2< Kernel > &r, const CGAL::Point_2< Kernel > &test)

members.name: side_of_oriented_sphere
members.brief: returns the relative position of point test to the oriented sphere defined by p , q , r and s .
members.detailed: The order of the points p , q , r , and s is important, since it determines the orientation of the implicitly constructed sphere. If the points p , q , r and s are positive oriented, positive side is the bounded interior of the sphere. In case of degeneracies, CGAL::ON_ORIENTED_BOUNDARY is returned if all points are coplanar. Otherwise, there is a cyclic permutation of the five points that puts four non coplanar points first, it is used to answer the predicate: e.g. CGAL::side_of_oriented_sphere(q, r, s, test, p) is returned if q , r , s , and test are non coplanar.
members.argsstring: (const CGAL::Point_3< Kernel > &p, const CGAL::Point_3< Kernel > &q, const CGAL::Point_3< Kernel > &r, const CGAL::Point_3< Kernel > &s, const CGAL::Point_3< Kernel > &test)

members.name: squared_distance
members.brief: computes the square of the Euclidean distance between two geometric objects.
members.detailed: For arbitrary geometric objects obj1 and obj2 the squared distance is defined as the minimal squared_distance(p1, p2) , where p1 is a point of obj1 and p2 is a point of obj2 . Note that for objects that have an inside (a bounded region), this inside is part of the object. So, the squared distance from a point inside is zero, not the squared distance to the closest point on the boundary. In 2D, the types Type1 and Type2 can be any of the following: Point_2 Line_2 Ray_2 Segment_2 Triangle_2 as well as any combination of Kernel::Point_2 and Kernel::Weighted_point_2 In 3D, the types Type1 and Type2 can be any of the following: Point_3 Line_3 Ray_3 Segment_3 Plane_3 as well as the following combinations: Point_3 / Triangle_3 Point_3 / Tetrahedron_3 Weighted_point_3 / Triangle_3 Weighted_point_3 / Tetrahedron_3 Triangle_3 / Triangle_3 CGAL::compare_distance_to_point() CGAL::compare_signed_distance_to_line() CGAL::compare_signed_distance_to_plane() CGAL::has_larger_distance_to_point() CGAL::has_larger_signed_distance_to_line() CGAL::has_larger_signed_distance_to_plane() CGAL::has_smaller_distance_to_point() CGAL::has_smaller_signed_distance_to_line() CGAL::has_smaller_signed_distance_to_plane()

PkgKernel23Ref.name: PkgKernel23Ref
PkgKernel23Ref.detailed: Hervé Brönnimann, Andreas Fabri, Geert-Jan Giezeman, Susan Hert, Michael Hoffmann, Lutz Kettner, Sylvain Pion, and Stefan Schirra This package contains kernels each containing objects of constant size, such as point, vector, direction, line, ray, segment, circle as well as predicates and constructions for these objects. The kernels mainly differ in the way they handle robustness issues. Introduced in: CGAL 0.9 BibTeX: cgal:bfghhkps-lgk23-26a License: LGPL Classified Reference Pages Concepts Kernel Kernel Classes and Operations CGAL::Cartesian <FieldNumberType> members.name: compare_angle
members.brief: compares the angles $ \theta_1$ and $ \theta_2$ , where $ \theta_1$ is the angle in $ [0, \pi]$ of the triangle $ (a, b, c)$ at the vertex b , and $ \theta_2$ is the angle in $ [0, \pi]$ such that $ cos(\theta_2) = cosine$ .
members.argsstring: (const CGAL::Point_3< Kernel > &a, const CGAL::Point_3< Kernel > &b, const CGAL::Point_3< Kernel > &c, const Kernel::FT &cosine)

members.name: compare_projection_along_direction_3
members.brief: returns CGAL::SMALLER , CGAL::EQUAL , or CGAL::LARGER if the projection of p onto a line with direction dir precedes, coincides, or follows that of q in the direction pointed by dir .
members.detailed: CGAL::compare_distance_to_point() CGAL::compare_signed_distance_to_line() CGAL::compare_signed_distance_to_plane() CGAL::has_larger_distance_to_point() CGAL::has_larger_signed_distance_to_line() CGAL::has_larger_signed_distance_to_plane() CGAL::has_smaller_distance_to_point() CGAL::has_smaller_signed_distance_to_line() CGAL::has_smaller_signed_distance_to_plane()
members.argsstring: (const CGAL::Point_3< Kernel > &p, const CGAL::Point_3< Kernel > &q, const CGAL::Direction_3< Kernel > &dir)

members.brief: A symbolic constant used to construct zero length vectors.

members.name: ORIGIN
members.brief: A symbolic constant which denotes the point at the origin.
members.detailed: This constant is used in the conversion between points and vectors. Example Point_2< Cartesian<Exact_NT> > p(1.0, 1.0), q; Vector2< Cartesian<Exact_NT> > v; v = p - ORIGIN ; q = ORIGIN + v; assert( p == q ); CGAL::Point_2 < Kernel > CGAL::Point_3 < Kernel >

members.name: enum_cast
members.brief: converts between the various enums provided by the CGAL kernel.
members.detailed: The conversion preserves the order of the values. CGAL::Sign CGAL::Comparison_result CGAL::Orientation CGAL::Oriented_side CGAL::Bounded_side CGAL::Angle CGAL::Uncertain<T>

members.name: opposite
members.brief: returns the opposite side (for example CGAL::ON_POSITIVE_SIDE if o == CGAL::ON_NEGATIVE_SIDE ), or CGAL::ON_ORIENTED_BOUNDARY if o == CGAL::ON_ORIENTED_BOUNDARY .

members.name: opposite
members.brief: returns the opposite side (for example CGAL::ON_BOUNDED_SIDE if o == CGAL::ON_UNBOUNDED_SIDE ), or returns CGAL::ON_BOUNDARY if o == CGAL::ON_BOUNDARY .

members.name: cartesian_to_homogeneous
members.brief: Functions to convert between Cartesian and homogeneous kernels.
members.detailed: CGAL::Cartesian <FieldNumberType> CGAL::Cartesian_converter <K1, K2, NTConverter> CGAL::Homogeneous <RingNumberType> CGAL::Homogeneous_converter <K1, K2, RTConverter, FTConverter> CGAL::Simple_cartesian <FieldNumberType> CGAL::Simple_homogeneous <RingNumberType> converts 2D point cp with Cartesian representation into a 2D point with homogeneous representation with the same number type.

members.name: cartesian_to_homogeneous
members.brief: converts 3D point cp with Cartesian representation into a 3D point with homogeneous representation with the same number type.

members.name: homogeneous_to_cartesian
members.brief: converts 2D point hp with homogeneous representation into a 2D point with Cartesian representation with the same number type.

members.name: homogeneous_to_cartesian
members.brief: converts 3D point hp with homogeneous representation into a 3D point with Cartesian representation with the same number type.

members.name: homogeneous_to_quotient_cartesian
members.brief: converts the 2D point hp with homogeneous representation with number type RT into a 2D point with Cartesian representation with number type Quotient<RT> .

members.name: homogeneous_to_quotient_cartesian
members.brief: converts the 3D point hp with homogeneous representation with number type RT into a 3D point with Cartesian representation with number type Quotient<RT> .

members.name: quotient_cartesian_to_homogeneous
members.brief: converts 2D point cp with Cartesian representation with number type Quotient<RT> into a 2D point with homogeneous representation with number type RT .

members.name: quotient_cartesian_to_homogeneous
members.brief: converts 3D point cp with Cartesian representation with number type Quotient<RT> into a 3D point with homogeneous representation with number type RT .
